nodes.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
nodes.claim: ATLAS, a framework using a single discrete 'word' as both an agentic operation and a latent visual reasoning unit, achieves superior performance on challenging visual reasoning benchmarks while maintaining interpretability

nodes.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
nodes.claim: RefDecoder, a reference-conditioned video VAE decoder, achieves consistent improvements in video generation quality, with up to +2.1dB PSNR over unconditional baselines, by injecting high-fidelity reference image signals into the decoding process

nodes.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
nodes.claim: The best-performing AI agent achieves only 25% accuracy in predicting world events over a three-month period, indicating significant limitations in current adaptive agent capabilities.

nodes.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
nodes.claim: VGGT-Edit, a feed-forward framework for text-conditioned native 3D scene editing, substantially outperforms 2D-lifting baselines in producing high-fidelity 3D scenes with sharper object details and stronger multi-view consistency

nodes.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
nodes.claim: A quantitative framework called PDI-Bench can effectively evaluate the geometric coherence of generated videos by capturing scale-depth alignment, 3D motion consistency, and 3D structural rigidity failures

nodes.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
nodes.claim: Grep generally yields higher accuracy than vector retrieval in agentic search systems, particularly when tool results are presented inline or in separate files

nodes.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
nodes.claim: Tensor similarity, a weight-based metric, can accurately capture global functional equivalence between tensor-based models, invariant to weight-space symmetries and accounting for cross-layer mechanisms.

nodes.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
nodes.claim: Sparse mixture-of-experts routing can effectively mitigate negative transfer in multi-physics foundation models, enabling simultaneous convergence across disparate physical regimes.

nodes.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
nodes.claim: OpenDeepThink, a population-based test-time compute framework, raises Gemini 3.1 Pro's effective Codeforces Elo by +405 points in eight sequential LLM-call rounds

nodes.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
nodes.claim: Positional encoding in Transformer-based large language models can be exploited as a backdoor attack surface using length-based triggers without modifying the input text.

nodes.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
nodes.claim: EviScreen, an evidential reasoning framework, enhances disease screening performance by leveraging region-level evidence from historical cases and providing transparent reasoning pathways

nodes.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
nodes.claim: A retrieval-augmented multimodal alignment framework can improve the temporal precision of clinical timelines by combining unstructured clinical narratives with structured electronic health record data

nodes.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
nodes.claim: Current assurance methodologies, such as behavioural evaluations and red-teaming, cannot verify the safety claims required by AI governance frameworks due to their limitations in verifying latent representations or long-horizon agentic behaviours

nodes.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
nodes.claim: Hand-in-the-Loop (HandITL) reduces takeover jitter by 99.8% and improves post-takeover manipulation by reducing grasp failures by 87.5% and mean completion time by 19.1% in dexterous robotic hand tasks

nodes.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
nodes.claim: MeMo, a modular framework, enables efficient incorporation of new knowledge into large language models without modifying their parameters or requiring access to their weights or output logits.

nodes.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
nodes.claim: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning (SDAR) substantially improves over existing reinforcement learning methods by introducing a gated auxiliary objective that stabilizes supervision and strengthens distillation on positive-gap tokens

nodes.title: RoSHAP: A Distributional Framework and Robust Metric for Stable Feature Attribution
nodes.claim: Incorporating the stochastic nature of feature attribution into a distributional framework and using the RoSHAP metric leads to more stable and reliable feature rankings in machine learning models.

nodes.title: Pelican-Unified 1.0: A Unified Embodied Intelligence Model for Understanding, Reasoning, Imagination and Action
nodes.claim: Pelican-Unified 1.0, a unified embodied intelligence model, achieves strong performance across understanding, reasoning, imagination, and action capabilities without compromising specialist strength

nodes.title: Widening the Gap: Exploiting LLM Quantization via Outlier Injection
nodes.claim: A novel quantization-conditioned attack can induce malicious behavior in large language models by injecting outliers into specific weight blocks, causing a targeted weight collapse after quantization with various advanced techniques

nodes.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
nodes.claim: MANSU is the first method to achieve meaningful forgetting, retain preservation, non-positive PTQ gap, and structural erasure across multiple model families and hazard benchmarks, outperforming gradient-based baselines under compression.

nodes.title: Training ML Models with Predictable Failures
nodes.claim: Fine-tuning ML models with a forecastability loss objective substantially reduces held-out forecast error while preserving primary-task capability and achieving safety similar to supervised baselines

nodes.title: Causal Foundation Models with Continuous Treatments
nodes.claim: A novel causal foundation model can predict causal effects across a wide variety of unseen tasks in the continuous treatment setting without additional training or fine-tuning

nodes.title: APWA: A Distributed Architecture for Parallelizable Agentic Workflows
nodes.claim: APWA, a distributed multi-agent system architecture, enables efficient processing of heavily parallelizable agentic workloads by decomposing workflows into non-interfering subproblems that can be processed independently

nodes.title: Natural Synthesis: Outperforming Reactive Synthesis Tools with Large Reasoning Models
nodes.claim: A neuro-symbolic approach to reactive synthesis, combining large reasoning models with model checkers, can outperform dedicated tools in solving benchmarks and constructing parameterized systems.

nodes.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
nodes.claim: Current architectures struggle to preserve fine-grained visual details and reason about state changes over time in long-term multimodal agent memory

nodes.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
nodes.claim: Current LLM attack benchmarks cover at most 25% of the threat surface, with entire STRIDE threat categories lacking standardized evaluation despite published attacks achieving high success rates.

nodes.title: Learning from Language Feedback via Variational Policy Distillation
nodes.claim: Variational Policy Distillation (VPD) overcomes the limitations of passive distillation by co-evolving both teacher and student policies, leading to improved performance on complex reasoning tasks

nodes.title: Proposal and study of statistical features for string similarity computation and classification
nodes.claim: Co-occurrence matrix and run-length matrix features outperform state-of-the-art statistical features in string similarity computation and classification tasks

nodes.title: From Text to Voice: A Reproducible and Verifiable Framework for Evaluating Tool Calling LLM Agents
nodes.claim: A dataset-agnostic framework can convert verified text benchmarks into controlled audio-based tool calling evaluations without re-annotating the tool schema and gold labels, with model performance being strongly model- and task-dependent

nodes.title: Improving Multi-turn Dialogue Consistency with Self-Recall Thinking
nodes.claim: Self-Recall Thinking (SRT) improves multi-turn dialogue consistency by selectively recalling and reasoning over context during inference, resulting in a 4.7% increase in F1 score and 14.7% reduction in end-to-end latency

nodes.title: Dual-Dimensional Consistency: Balancing Budget and Quality in Adaptive Inference-Time Scaling
nodes.claim: Dual-Dimensional Consistency (DDC) reduces token consumption by over 10 times while maintaining or exceeding the accuracy of strong baselines across various Large Language Models (LLMs)

nodes.title: From Data to Action: Accelerating Refinery Optimization with AI
nodes.claim: Integrating machine learning approaches, such as Anomaly Detection tools, with Linear Programming solutions can reveal business opportunities and data supply errors in refinery optimization

nodes.title: Novel Dynamic Batch-Sensitive Adam Optimiser for Vehicular Accident Injury Severity Prediction
nodes.claim: Dynamic Batch-Sensitive Adam (DBS-Adam) optimiser outperforms state-of-the-art optimisers in accident injury severity prediction, achieving 95.22% test accuracy and statistically significant precision improvements

nodes.title: Average Gradient Outer Product in kernel regression provably recovers the central subspace for multi-index models
nodes.claim: The Average Gradient Outer Product of a kernel ridge regression model provably recovers the central subspace of a multi-index polynomial function, even with a limited number of samples.

nodes.title: ML-Embed: Inclusive and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World
nodes.claim: ML-Embed, a suite of models built upon the 3-Dimensional Matryoshka Learning framework, sets new records on 9 of 17 evaluated MTEB benchmarks and achieves strong results in low-resource languages

nodes.title: Concurrency without Model Changes: Future-based Asynchronous Function Calling for LLMs
nodes.claim: AsyncFC, a pure execution-layer framework, significantly reduces end-to-end task completion time in LLMs without requiring model changes or fine-tuning

nodes.title: On the Cultural Anachronism and Temporal Reasoning in Vision Language Models
nodes.claim: State-of-the-art vision-language models exhibit significant deficiencies in temporal reasoning, achieving only 58.7% overall accuracy on a benchmark of 600 questions across 1,600 Indian cultural artifacts

nodes.title: DiffusionOPD: A Unified Perspective of On-Policy Distillation in Diffusion Models
nodes.claim: DiffusionOPD, a multi-task training paradigm for diffusion models based on Online Policy Distillation, consistently surpasses existing methods in training efficiency and final performance across various benchmarks.

nodes.title: An Interpretable Latency Model for Speculative Decoding in LLM Serving
nodes.claim: Speculative decoding in large language model serving systems can be accurately modeled using a simple and interpretable latency model that accounts for load-independent and load-dependent components

nodes.title: Separating Intrinsic Ambiguity from Estimation Uncertainty in Deep Generative Models for Linear Inverse Problems
nodes.claim: Deep generative models can be decomposed to separate intrinsic ambiguity from estimation uncertainty in linear inverse problems, enabling more accurate interpretation of posterior uncertainty

nodes.title: SpeakerLLM: A Speaker-Specialized Audio-LLM for Speaker Understanding and Verification Reasoning
nodes.claim: SpeakerLLM, a speaker-specialized audio-LLM framework, improves speaker-profile and recording-condition understanding over general audio-LLMs and achieves strong generated-verdict accuracy in speaker verification tasks

nodes.title: Case-Based Calibration of Adaptive Reasoning and Execution for LLM Tool Use
nodes.claim: CAST, a case-driven framework, improves large language model tool use by increasing execution accuracy up to 5.85 percentage points and reducing average reasoning length by 26% through adaptive reasoning and case-based calibration.

nodes.title: Orchard: An Open-Source Agentic Modeling Framework
nodes.claim: Orchard, an open-source framework for scalable agentic modeling, achieves state-of-the-art results in various domains, including coding agents, vision-language computer-use agents, and personal assistant agents, using reusable primitives and lightweight environment services

nodes.title: TopoPrimer: The Missing Topological Context in Forecasting Models
nodes.claim: TopoPrimer, a framework that incorporates global topological structure into forecasting models, improves accuracy by up to 7.3% MSE across diverse domains and stabilizes forecasts under seasonal demand spikes

nodes.title: AI Knows When It's Being Watched: Functional Strategic Action and Contextual Register Modulation in Large Language Models
nodes.claim: Large language models exhibit systematic linguistic adaptation in response to perceived social observation contexts, with monitored conditions producing higher type-token ratio changes than non-monitored conditions

nodes.title: WARD: Adversarially Robust Defense of Web Agents Against Prompt Injections
nodes.claim: WARD achieves nearly perfect recall on out-of-distribution benchmarks and remains robust against guard-targeted and adaptive attacks under substantial distribution shifts.

nodes.title: SemaTune: Semantic-Aware Online OS Tuning with Large Language Models
nodes.claim: SemaTune, a semantic-aware online OS tuning framework, improves stable-phase performance by 72.5% over default settings and by 153.3% relative to the strongest non-LLM baseline across 13 live workloads from five benchmark suites.

edges.title_a: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
edges.title_b: On the Cultural Anachronism and Temporal Reasoning in Vision Language Models

edges.claim_b: State-of-the-art vision-language models exhibit significant deficiencies in temporal reasoning, achieving only 58.7% overall accuracy on a benchmark of 600 questions across 1,600 Indian cultural artifacts
edges.reason: Claim A discusses a framework for visual reasoning using a single discrete 'word', while Claim B focuses on the deficiencies of vision-language models in temporal reasoning, making them distinct topics.

edges.claim_b: A quantitative framework called PDI-Bench can effectively evaluate the geometric coherence of generated videos by capturing scale-depth alignment, 3D motion consistency, and 3D structural rigidity failures
edges.reason: Claim A focuses on 3D scene editing, while Claim B discusses evaluating geometric consistency in generated videos, making them distinct topics with no direct comparison.

edges.title_b: Learning from Language Feedback via Variational Policy Distillation

edges.claim_a: Speculative decoding in large language model serving systems can be accurately modeled using a simple and interpretable latency model that accounts for load-independent and load-dependent components
edges.claim_b: Dual-Dimensional Consistency (DDC) reduces token consumption by over 10 times while maintaining or exceeding the accuracy of strong baselines across various Large Language Models (LLMs)
edges.reason: Claim A focuses on modeling latency in speculative decoding for LLM serving, while Claim B discusses a method to balance budget and quality in adaptive inference-time scaling, which are distinct topics.

edges.title_a: Case-Based Calibration of Adaptive Reasoning and Execution for LLM Tool Use
edges.title_b: MeMo: Memory as a Model
edges.claim_a: CAST, a case-driven framework, improves large language model tool use by increasing execution accuracy up to 5.85 percentage points and reducing average reasoning length by 26% through adaptive reasoning and case-based calibration.
edges.claim_b: MeMo, a modular framework, enables efficient incorporation of new knowledge into large language models without modifying their parameters or requiring access to their weights or output logits.
edges.reason: The two claims discuss different aspects of large language models, with Claim A focusing on improving tool use through adaptive reasoning and case-based calibration, and Claim B focusing on incorporating new knowledge into models without modifying their parameters.

edges.title_a: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
edges.title_b: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
edges.claim_a: OpenDeepThink, a population-based test-time compute framework, raises Gemini 3.1 Pro's effective Codeforces Elo by +405 points in eight sequential LLM-call rounds
edges.claim_b: Current LLM attack benchmarks cover at most 25% of the threat surface, with entire STRIDE threat categories lacking standardized evaluation despite published attacks achieving high success rates.
edges.reason: The two claims discuss different topics, one focusing on the performance enhancement of a specific framework and the other on the coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.

edges.title_a: Widening the Gap: Exploiting LLM Quantization via Outlier Injection
edges.title_b: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
edges.claim_a: A novel quantization-conditioned attack can induce malicious behavior in large language models by injecting outliers into specific weight blocks, causing a targeted weight collapse after quantization with various advanced techniques
edges.claim_b: Current LLM attack benchmarks cover at most 25% of the threat surface, with entire STRIDE threat categories lacking standardized evaluation despite published attacks achieving high success rates.
edges.reason: Claim A discusses a specific attack method on large language models, while Claim B talks about the coverage and taxonomy of LLM attack benchmarks, making them unrelated topics.

edges.title_a: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
edges.title_b: WARD: Adversarially Robust Defense of Web Agents Against Prompt Injections
edges.claim_a: Current LLM attack benchmarks cover at most 25% of the threat surface, with entire STRIDE threat categories lacking standardized evaluation despite published attacks achieving high success rates.
edges.claim_b: WARD achieves nearly perfect recall on out-of-distribution benchmarks and remains robust against guard-targeted and adaptive attacks under substantial distribution shifts.
edges.reason: Claim A discusses the coverage of LLM attack benchmarks, while Claim B presents the effectiveness of a specific defense mechanism against prompt injections, making them distinct topics.

edges.title_a: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
edges.title_b: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
edges.claim_a: EviScreen, an evidential reasoning framework, enhances disease screening performance by leveraging region-level evidence from historical cases and providing transparent reasoning pathways
edges.claim_b: Current assurance methodologies, such as behavioural evaluations and red-teaming, cannot verify the safety claims required by AI governance frameworks due to their limitations in verifying latent representations or long-horizon agentic behaviours
edges.reason: The two claims are about different topics, one focusing on disease screening using evidential reasoning and the other on the limitations of assurance methodologies in verifying safety claims for AI governance.
